title: AWS Continuum to Enable Agentic Code Security for Enterprises
gist: AWS launched Continuum, an integrated agentic security platform covering penetration testing, code review, threat modelling, and code-vulnerability remediation with a graduated trust model, but practitioners are already flagging confusing product overlap with the six-month-old AWS Security Agent, and AWS's ecosystem-locked approach contrasts with Google's cloud-agnostic strategy in the same emerging agentic-security category.

insights.claim: AWS Continuum launches with four agentic capabilities targeting the entire vulnerability lifecycle -- penetration testing, code review, threat modelling, and code vulnerabilities -- with the code-vulnerabilities capability operating through four continuous phases: discovery, prioritisation, validation, and mitigation/remediation.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: Defines the feature set enterprises should expect from AWS's flagship agentic security offering and the bar competitors need to match.
insights.quote: Continuum is built on lessons learned from running security across AWS and Amazon.com
insights.locator: Chet Kapoor, vice president of search, security, and observability at AWS

insights.claim: The code-vulnerabilities capability reasons over a company's full environment -- including structured and unstructured data such as infrastructure, permissions, network topology, documents, communications, and business priorities -- and builds fully functional exploit examples in a sandboxed environment to reduce false positives with real evidence of exploitability.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: Shows agentic security tooling moving toward automated exploit validation as the mechanism for cutting false-positive noise, a capability that could reshape vulnerability-management vendor differentiation.
insights.locator: During the prioritisation and validation phases

insights.claim: The code-vulnerabilities capability uses a graduated trust model letting security and product teams choose how much autonomy to delegate for analysis, remediation, and enforcement, based on user-defined risk categories and profiles.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: Reflects broader industry caution about granting security agents full autonomy for remediation actions -- graduated trust/approval gating is becoming a standard design pattern.
insights.locator: According to the company, the capability operates using a graduated trust model

themes.theme: Product overlap and rapid rebranding risk

insights.claim: AWS's penetration testing and code review capabilities, originally launched as AWS Security Agent at re:Invent 2025, were folded into and rebranded as "Continuum pen testing" and "Continuum code scanning" only six months later, while the original Security Agent product page and capabilities still exist separately, creating two product names for the same functionality with no stated plan for whether they will diverge.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: A concrete example of AWS's fast-moving agentic-product iteration creating buyer confusion, which could slow enterprise adoption or trigger vendor consolidation scrutiny.
insights.quote: So now we have the same capabilities, two product names, and no clarity on which one you should use or whether they will diverge at some point. This is just confusing.

insights.claim: AWS is not alone in building agentic security remediation for enterprises -- Google (AI Threat Defense) and Microsoft (MDASH) offer competing capabilities, but Google has prioritized a cloud-agnostic strategy allowing scans across diverse cloud environments, while Microsoft and AWS have opted for architectures more tightly integrated within their own ecosystems.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: Identifies a strategic fork in the emerging agentic-security market between cloud-agnostic (Google) and ecosystem-locked (AWS, Microsoft) go-to-market approaches, relevant for evaluating vendor lock-in risk versus multi-cloud security tooling investment.
insights.locator: AWS isn't the only company exploring the landscape

insights.claim: Only penetration testing and code review are generally available with established pricing; threat modelling remains in preview and code-vulnerabilities remains in gated preview requiring sign-up, indicating AWS is still validating the reliability of its more autonomous remediation capabilities before wide release.
insights.speaker: Gianmarco Nalin
insights.so_what: A concrete maturity signal for buyers evaluating how production-ready AWS's most autonomous security capabilities actually are today.
insights.locator: Penetration testing and code-review capabilities are already generally available
